pr.title: Sync motion enum names to submotion names
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Renamed fighter motion-state enum constants across many character forward headers so the ft*_MS_* names line up with the corresponding submotion names, then updated source references in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, motion_id switches, and boss motion tables. The PR was a broad naming/header synchronization change with no body text or review discussion in the dump; the evidence points to readability and enum consistency rather than an intended behavior change.

key_files.reason: Largest header churn; Kirby motion-state enum names were synced to submotion naming.
Replaced many ftMh_MS_Unk*/StartMotion-style names with descriptive submotion-aligned names such as Wait1_0, Entry, Damage2, SweepLoop, RockCrushUp, FingerBeamStart, Squeeze, Throw, and tag states.
Updated many Master Hand motion_id comparisons and Fighter_ChangeMotionState references to the new enum names.
Updated a Crazy Hand table of Master Hand motion-state IDs from unknown/start-motion labels to descriptive names.
Renamed Donkey Kong motion states from Unk/Cargo/Charge terminology to submotion-like Heavy, ThrowF, SpecialNStart/Loop/Cancel/Full, and SpecialLwEnd0/1 names.
Updated many Donkey Kong SpecialN transition references after the enum rename.
Shows the core naming pattern: item swing and special-state names were changed from older underscore/number labels to submotion-aligned names.
Added a small ftSb_MotionState enum with ftSb_MS_WaitReverse = ftCo_MS_Count to mirror the existing ftSb_SM_WaitReverse submotion.

what_changed: Changed 65 fighter files with 803 added and 859 deleted lines, mostly enum constant renames and reference updates.
Synchronized motion-state enum names in character forward.h files to match submotion naming rather than older unknown, numeric, or underscored names.
Updated call sites in source files that pass the renamed constants to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Updated motion_id switch cases and comparisons to use renamed constants, for example Captain Falcon SpecialSStart/SpecialAirSStart and Game & Watch SpecialLwCatch/SpecialLwShoot.

Renamed Captain Falcon item swing states from Swing42_* style to concrete item/submotion-style names such as ftCa_MS_SwordSwing4, ftCa_MS_BatSwing4, ftCa_MS_ParasolSwing4, ftCa_MS_HarisenSwing4, ftCa_MS_StarRodSwing4, and ftCa_MS_LipstickSwing4.

Renamed Captain Falcon special state suffixes by removing separator underscores and aligning with submotion words: SpecialS_Start to SpecialSStart, SpecialHi_Catch to SpecialHiCatch, SpecialLw_GroundEnd to SpecialLwEnd, and related air/end variants.
Renamed Donkey Kong's unknown and Cargo-prefixed motion states to Heavy and ThrowF names, and shortened SpecialN charge/release states to Start, Loop, Cancel, normal, and Full forms.
Renamed many Master Hand motion states from ftMh_MS_Unk* and *_StartMotion forms to descriptive labels, and propagated those names into Master Hand and Crazy Hand initialization/table code.
Added a Sandbag motion-state enum entry ftSb_MS_WaitReverse aligned with ftSb_SM_WaitReverse.
smart_moves: Kept the refactor mechanical: enum declarations and all dependent references changed together, reducing the risk of stale identifiers.
Used existing submotion names as the naming source of truth, which makes motion-state IDs easier to correlate with animation/submotion data.
Converted unknown-numbered boss motion states to descriptive names in one pass, improving later decomp readability for Master Hand and Crazy Hand code.
Preserved enum ordering and ftCo_MS_Count anchoring while changing names, which is important because motion-state numeric values are ABI/data-table sensitive.
Updated table initializers as well as direct Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, catching non-obvious references such as Crazy Hand's ftCh_Init_803D4878 asids array.
decomp_lessons: For fighter motion enums, prefer syncing ft*_MS_* names with ft*_SM_* submotion names when the mapping is known; this makes code/data cross-reference work easier.
Motion-state renames should be propagated through headers, transitions, motion_id switches, comparisons, and initializer tables in the same PR to keep the build coherent.

Do not assume function names must change when enum names change; this PR renamed constants but left functions such as ftDk_SpecialNChargeStart_Anim intact where the diff evidence only supports enum cleanup.
Boss/shared-fighter code can contain cross-character dependencies: Crazy Hand code referenced Master Hand motion states, so a Master Hand enum rename required Crazy Hand updates too.
When replacing UnkNNN enum names, maintain the original enum order and only improve the symbolic label; the underlying numeric motion ID must remain stable.
naming_conventions: Use character prefix plus MS for motion states, e.g. ftDk_MS_SpecialNStart and ftMh_MS_FingerBeamStart.
Use submotion-style compound words without extra separator underscores for action phases, e.g. SpecialSStart, SpecialLwCatch, SpecialAirLwShoot, SpecialHiThrow.
Prefer descriptive item/action names over numeric placeholders, e.g. SwordSwing4, BatSwing4, ParasolSwing4 instead of Swing42_Sword and Swing42_2.
Prefer known action names over UnkNNN where submotion evidence exists, e.g. ftMh_MS_Unk345 became ftMh_MS_Damage2.
For paired or repeated variants, numeric suffixes remain acceptable when present in submotion names, e.g. Wait1_0, Wait1_1, SpecialLwEnd0, SpecialLwEnd1.
For Donkey Kong carry-related states, this PR moved away from Cargo* labels toward Heavy* and ThrowF* labels matching submotion terminology.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The rename should not affect code generation if enum values and ordering remain unchanged; it is a safe readability refactor for matching work.
Updating enum identifiers in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls can improve matcher/debugger clarity without changing the numeric msid arguments.
For large enum renames, search all direct uses of the old identifiers, including initializer arrays and motion_id comparisons, not just transition calls.
When matching fighter code, compare ft*_MotionState and ft*_Submotion enum order/name pairs to identify better names for unknown motion states.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the dump slice.
The PR body was empty, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff rather than explicit author/reviewer explanation.
searchable_terms: Sync motion enum names to submotion names
follow_up_queries: Find remaining ft*_MS_Unk motion-state enum names that have matching ft*_SM_* submotion names.
Audit fighter forward.h files for motion-state and submotion enum divergence after PR 818.
Search for StartMotion or underscore-separated motion-state names that should follow submotion naming.
Check cross-character boss code for Master Hand motion-state references after enum renames.
